title: Frequently Asked Questions Overview and Search Guide
text: Frequently asked questions Discover more about the campus, student life and our programmes by seeing what detail others have asked us in the past. You might also want to know Type a word or phrase into the searchbox to filter the complete list of questions. When you see the question you want answered, just click the + (plus) icon to the left of the question to find out more. Search FAQs
summary: This section introduces the Frequently Asked Questions page, guiding users on how to search and navigate questions related to campus, student life, and academic programs.

title: General Scholarship and Bursary Application Guide
text: Can I apply for a scholarship or bursary? We welcome applications for a scholarship or bursary from any self-funding student who will be paying domestic Tuition Fees. Additional criteria, such as programme eligibility, are shown clearly on individual award pages. You must first make an application to study a specific programme before you make an application for a scholarship or bursary. How can I contribute to the community as a scholarship student? We would encourage - and expect - that the recipient of a scholarship or bursary will act as a Student Ambassador to promote University of Southampton Delhi while they study with us. How would I receive my scholarship or bursary? There is no cash alternative to the waiver of Tuition Fees made under a scholarship or bursary. We will reduce the amount of Tuition Fees that you will pay by the value of the award. What are the terms and conditions for scholarships? You can see our current terms and conditions for scholarships and bursaries here. What is a merit bursary? A merit bursary is a form of financial aid for students who otherwise would not be able to fund their education. It is based on the financial ability of students to support educational aims as well, as merit criteria based on academic performance. Will I lose my deposit if I get a full scholarship? If you are awarded a full scholarship - which waives 100% of Tuition Fees - we will refund the value of any Deposit that you may have already paid.
summary: This section explains general eligibility, application requirements, and the benefits for scholarships and bursaries, including terms, merit bursary definition, and deposit refunds.

title: Specific Scholarship Awards: Cambridge and L.E.K.
text: Is there an application fee waiver for Cambridge students applying to the University of Southampton Delhi? Yes, all Cambridge students applying to any undergraduate programme at University of Southampton Delhi between 01 May and 31 May 2025 will have their application fees waived. Who is eligible to apply for the Cambridge Scholarship Awards? The Cambridge Scholarship Awards are open to all Cambridge A-Level graduates in India who are applying to selected undergraduate programmes at University of Southampton Delhi. What additional benefits are available to L.E.K. Scholars? In addition to the scholarship, recipients of the L.E.K. Scholar Award will receive priority consideration for L.E.K. Consulting’s Summer Internship Programme. This provides an excellent opportunity for hands-on experience and professional growth, subject to performance and eligibility. Who is eligible to apply for an L.E.K. Scholar Award? Students with an unconditional offer to study for BSc (Hons) Economics or BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance can be considered for an L.E.K. Scholar award. Why should I apply for an L.E.K. Scholar Award? This scholarship will represent a highly prestigious accolade for our top students, aligned with one of the world’s most respected strategy consulting firms and offering an outstanding pathway into top-tier global career opportunities.
summary: This section outlines eligibility and benefits for specific scholarships, including application fee waivers for Cambridge A-Level graduates and L.E.K. Scholar Award benefits like internship priority and career opportunities.

title: General Admissions Process and Application Information
text: Can I get an offer with predicted scores? We encourage students to apply as soon as possible so that they can receive a Conditional Offer based on predicted scores for their chosen programme. We can accept an application from a student using their predicted grades if we receive their final grades once they are available. How much does an application to study cost? There is normally a 2,000 INR application assessment fee which students would need to pay in order for us to process their application for those starting in August 2026. Should I wait to make my application to study? We encourage students to make their application to study at University of Southampton Delhi as soon as they can. This means we can issue an early Conditional Offer and that students will have the best the chance to meet their offer conditions. If demand is high, we may choose to close applications earlier than mentioned, but students with Offers to study would receive preferential treatment. When will the next academic year start? You can see the most up to date information about start dates on our page showing key information.
summary: This section covers applying with predicted scores, the application fee for August 2026 intake, the benefits of early application for conditional offers, and where to find academic year start dates.

title: International Student Admissions and Visa Regulations
text: How do I apply as an international student? You need to complete the application form, upload the required documents (including certified English translations of transcripts), and pay an application fee of INR 5,500. For programme-specific requirements, please check the relevant programme page. Who is considered an international student at University of Southampton Delhi? A student who does not hold an Indian passport is considered an international student. However, Overseas Citizens of India (OCI) are classified under the Indian fee structure. What are the tuition fees for international students? International tuition fees are listed separately for each programme. OCI cardholders are subject to the Indian student fee rates. Do I need a visa to study at University of Southampton Delhi? As an international student, you must apply for a Student Visa endorsed to University of Southampton after receiving your offer letter and completing your tuition deposit. Please apply through the official Indian visa portal. Can international students work while studying in India? Currently, the Indian student visa does not permit part-time employment. You are advised to check the most recent government guidelines or speak to the International Student Office for updated information. What is FRRO registration and how do I complete it? FRRO (Foreigners Regional Registration Office) registration is mandatory for international students and must be completed within 14 days of arrival in India. The university will assist you in initiating this process.
summary: This section outlines the application process, definition, tuition fees, visa requirements, work restrictions, and mandatory FRRO registration for international students at University of Southampton Delhi.

title: Required Documents for Postgraduate and Undergraduate Applications
text: What documents do I need to apply for a Postgraduate Master's programme? Academic Certificates (Certificates from previous study) Students who are still to complete their final year in college, are required to provide Predicted Achievement i.e. the predicted score from your college. You will be required to provide the Final Certificates as soon as available. Academic Transcripts (transcripts from previous study) Proof of English Language proficiency You can see more guidance about English Language on our website. If you have successfully completed a degree taught and assessed in English up to two years preceding the date of first enrolment at the University of Southampton, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Personal Statement (250-500 words) You can see more guidance about Personal Statements on our website. We suggest you keep a draft version ready, explaining your motivation to study your subject of interest at Southampton, and how you intend to pursue a career in the area. Letter of Reference Photo-Identity Card (such as Adhaar card/passport) Resume or Curriculum Vitae (often known as a CV) What documents do I need to apply for an Undergraduate Bachelor's programme? Academic Certificates (certificates from Grade XII and Grade X) Students who are yet to complete their final year in school are required to provide Predicted Achievement i.e. the predicted score from your school. Please ensure that your predicted subject scores are specified in the document. Academic Transcripts (transcripts from Grade XII and Grade X) Proof of English Language proficiency You can see more guidance about English Language on our website. If you have been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final four years of high school, you may choose to use your English scores to meet the English Language requirement. Personal Statement (250-500 words) You can see more guidance about Personal Statements on our website. We suggest you keep a draft version ready, explaining your motivation to study your subject of interest at Southampton, and how you intend to pursue a career in the area. Letter of Reference Photo-Identity Card (such as Aadhaar card/passport) What level of English proficiency is required? All programmes are taught in English. You must meet the university’s English language requirements, either through school-level qualifications or by submitting a valid score from an accepted English proficiency test such as IELTS, TOEFL, or equivalent.
summary: This section details required application documents for both postgraduate and undergraduate programs, including academic certificates, transcripts, predicted scores, English language proficiency, personal statements, references, and identification.

title: Programme Structure, Academic Standards and Student Capacity
text: How can I earn an Honours degree in just 3 years? The University Grants Commission (UGC) has mandated University of Southampton Delhi to make sure that programmes delivered in India are the same as those delivered at University of Southampton in the UK. Three years is the standard length of an undergraduate Honours degree in England where University of Southampton sets academic standards. This 3 year length reflects a trend towards accelerated learning where students see a return on their tuition fees as quickly as possible, whilst still maintaining high academic standards and the quality of the degree awarded. University of Southampton 3 year undergraduate degrees, and 1 year postgraduate degrees, are accepted widely internationally. Why is an Honours degree good for me? An Honours degree, shown with (Hons) in the programme title, mean that students will understand the fundamentals of their discipline and then build specialisation and perspective through optional modules. These fast-paced, intense programmes deliver essential learning in the first academic year and offer optional course modules, or electives, in the second year. Honours degrees are offered at University of Southampton campuses across the world, reflecting teaching and learning to a higher academic standard, meaning that an Honours degree would hold more value post graduation for future study and career development. What is an option? Our programmes are designed to make sure that students quickly understand the fundamentals of their discipline, and then build specialisation and perspective through electives, or optional, modules. These optional modules are typically offered in academic years 2 and 3, adding depth and breadth to core learning from previous years. As these options are often built around real-world trends, the specific choice of module available to students from year to year may vary. Why does a dissertation include research? In a dissertation module, students complete an independent research project. The objective is for students to take their learning, and build something of their own, demonstrating the skills that they have acquired, and the independence that they will need in their future career. The research orientation is an intrinsic part of an Honours degree. Why is my degree award BSc (Hons) or BEng (Hons)? Degrees with a BA and BSc award do not represent a stream, but a broad categorisation that reflects student choice and leaning. Our BSc (Hons) and BEng (Hons) undergraduate degrees are oriented towards specialist, technical roles in industry with an analytical approach, not science subjects. What is the academic pass mark? The pass mark for all programmes within University of Southampton Delhi is 40%. This is the same pass mark across all University of Southampton campuses and reflects the high and equivalent academic standards across all our campuses. How many students will study on each programme? We expect to provide 25 seats on each undergraduate programme, and 20 seats for postgraduate programme, during academic year 25/26. We will provide seats number for academic year 26/27 soon.
summary: This section explains the 3-year Honours degree structure, optional modules, dissertation requirements, the meaning of BSc/BEng awards, academic pass marks, and student capacity for undergraduate and postgraduate programs.

title: Campus Visits, Accommodation, and Student Travel
text: Can I visit the campus in person or book a tour? Our campus is located at International Tech Park Gurgaon, Near Sec 59, Off, Golf Course Ext Rd, Gurugram, Haryana 122101. The best way to see campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page. How can I travel between the student hostel and campus? Perhaps the best way to get to campus in the morning from our student hostel, or back there again at night, is the private shuttle bus. This free service will run at regular intervals every weekday. When can I book a room at the student hostel? You can apply for a room at the student hostel when you have completed your application to study. We expect demand for rooms to be high, but you can express interest in accommodation for free, which which does not commit you to any contract. We will be in touch with those who have shown interest in accommodation to advise on availability and how to book a room.
summary: This section provides information on visiting the campus, its location, the private shuttle bus service for travel between the hostel and campus, and how to express interest in student accommodation.

title: Understanding Education Loans for Study Costs
text: What is an education loan? An education loan is a private commercial agreement between a student (or their family) with a financial institution to cover the costs of education. These independent organisations are not part of University of Southampton Delhi, any links are provided for information only and should not be taken as a recommendation. It is important to take financial and legal advice before entering into any agreement. Application for an education loan will not guarantee an offer to study, or enrolment on a programme, at University of Southampton Delhi.
summary: This section defines an education loan as a private commercial agreement for covering education costs, emphasizing its independence from the university and the need for legal and financial advice.

title: Meeting the Team and General Contact Information
text: How can I meet your team? You can meet us online, and in a selection of major cities in India. You can see when and where, by visiting the Events area of our website. When event tickets are available, you will be able to book a physical event, or to register for an on online session, from the event page. The best way to see campus is to book a pre-planned tour from our events page. Did you still want to know more? If you did not find the answer you wanted, get in touch and ask us your question. We will reply to you individually, so please make sure you leave an email and a phone number where we can contact you. Get in touch
summary: This section explains how to meet the university team through online or in-person events in India and encourages direct contact for any further questions not covered in the FAQs.
